Stucco Repair in Llano County, TX

Out in Llano County, stucco surfaces endure some of the most intense UV exposure in the Central Texas region. Pair that sun with rocky terrain, seasonal storms, and the humidity around Lake LBJ, and you have a recipe for cracking, chalking, and water damage. Star Stucco of Austin provides thorough, lasting repairs across Llano, Kingsland, Sunrise Beach, and the surrounding areas.

Get a Free EstimateCall (512) 706-9699

Llano County's Unique Stucco Challenges

Llano County sits on the Llano Uplift, one of the oldest exposed geological formations in Texas. The granite and gneiss bedrock means homes here are typically built on shallow, well-drained soils rather than the deep clay found to the east. While this reduces foundation movement, it creates its own stucco challenges — fast water runoff during storms can splash against walls, and the rocky terrain makes it difficult to maintain consistent grading around foundations.

The low humidity during summer combined with extreme heat accelerates UV damage to stucco. Surfaces that looked fine a few years ago can develop a chalky, weathered appearance as the binders break down. Left unaddressed, this leads to hairline cracks that let moisture in during the heavy rain events that punctuate long dry spells.

Many Llano County properties are weekend homes and lake houses near Lake LBJ and Inks Lake. These homes can sit unoccupied for weeks at a time, allowing small stucco issues to grow into larger problems before anyone notices. Our team provides thorough inspections and durable repairs designed for the realities of part-time and full-time Hill Country living.

What's Included With Our Stucco Repair in Llano County

Every project includes expert craftsmanship, quality materials, and a commitment to lasting results.

Complete Exterior Assessment

We inspect all stucco surfaces, paying particular attention to south- and west-facing walls that take the brunt of Llano County's intense sun.

Moisture Detection

We test behind the stucco at crack locations and areas with staining to determine if water has penetrated the wall system.

Failed Material Removal

We remove all stucco that has lost its bond, chalked through, or been compromised by moisture, down to sound substrate.

Substrate Repair

Damaged lath, building paper, and sheathing are repaired so the new stucco application has a solid, stable base.

Custom Color & Texture Match

We match the color and replicate the surface texture of your existing stucco so the repair is virtually invisible once cured.

Protective Finish Application

The repair is topped with a UV-resistant seal that slows the sun-driven degradation common on Llano County exteriors.

Common Stucco Repair Needs in Llano County

Here are the stucco issues we encounter most often on Llano County properties, shaped by the area's geology, sun exposure, and weather patterns.

Window & Door Frame Cracking

Thermal expansion from daily heat cycles stresses stucco at window and door openings. We rebuild these areas with reinforced mesh and materials that flex with temperature changes.

Water Intrusion Repair

Sudden heavy rains overwhelm stucco that has developed even minor cracks. We seal the entry points, dry the wall, and rebuild a watertight surface.

Failed Patch Repair

Previous patches that were applied with incompatible material or without adequate prep tend to crack and peel in the Hill Country sun. We strip the old work and start with a proper repair.

Delamination from Heat Cycling

Repeated expansion and contraction causes stucco to pull away from the lath. We remove the loose sections and rebuild them with improved mechanical bonding.

Sun-Driven Chalking & Fading

UV breakdown is accelerated by the open, unshaded terrain in much of Llano County. We repair the degraded surface and apply a sealer to slow future damage.

Impact & Animal Damage

Rural properties are susceptible to damage from wildlife, fallen limbs, and equipment. We restore chipped, gouged, and cracked areas to their original appearance.

Why Choose Us for Stucco Repair in Llano County?

  • We identify the actual cause of every crack and stain so the repair addresses the problem at its source.
  • Our finishing materials include UV-resistant additives formulated for the intense sun exposure in Llano County.
  • Every repair is custom color and texture matched for a seamless result.
  • We fix what is happening behind the stucco — moisture, lath failure, drainage — before covering it back up.
  • All repairs include a written warranty covering materials and workmanship.
  • Free inspections for Llano County properties — call (512) 706-9699.

Licensed & Insured

Full liability coverage and workers' compensation for every project in Llano County.

20+ Years Experience

Decades of hands-on stucco work across Central Texas residential and commercial properties.

Certified Applicators

Certified by Sto Corp, Senergy/BASF, Master Wall, Dryvit, and Parex LaHabra for the highest quality results.

Our Stucco Repair Process

A straightforward approach from start to finish — no surprises, no shortcuts.

1

Set Up Your Inspection

Call or submit a request online. We travel to all parts of Llano County, including Kingsland, Sunrise Beach, and properties along the Highland Lakes.

2

Pinpoint the Damage

Our technician inspects the stucco, checks for hidden moisture, and identifies the underlying cause — whether UV degradation, thermal cracking, or water intrusion.

3

Review the Estimate

We present a written repair plan with clear pricing. You see exactly what work is needed and what it will cost, with no hidden fees.

4

Get It Fixed Right

We remove the damaged stucco, repair the substrate, and apply a sealed, color-matched finish built to withstand Llano County conditions.
